sourceMovie.title: The Last Boy Scout
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Last Boy Scout for the same reason it broke through in 1991: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Tony Scott keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Bad Boys
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Last Boy Scout clicked for you, Bad Boys (1995) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Point Break
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Last Boy Scout clicked for you, Point Break (1991) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Last Boy Scout?
faq.answer: Try Bad Boys, Point Break, The Kingdom—each shares DNA with The Last Boy Scout while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Last Boy Scout?
faq.answer: The Last Boy Scout (1991) is best described as Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Last Boy Scout worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
